2604.11373.title: Minimal Embodiment Enables Efficient Learning of Number Concepts in Robot
2604.11373.paper_summary: Robots are increasingly entering human-interactive scenarios that require understanding of quantity. How intelligent systems acquire abstract numerical concepts from sensorimotor experience remains a fundamental challenge in cognitive science and artificial intelligence. Here we investigate embodied numerical learning using a neural network model trained to perform sequential counting through naturalistic robotic interaction with a Franka Panda manipulator. We demonstrate that embodied models achieve 96.8\% counting accuracy with only 10\% of training data, compared to 60.6\% for vision-only baselines. This advantage persists when visual-motor correspondences are randomized, indicating that embodiment functions as a structural prior that regularizes learning rather than as an information source. The model spontaneously develops biologically plausible representations: number-selective units with logarithmic tuning, mental number line organization, Weber-law scaling, and rotational dynamics encoding numerical magnitude ($r = 0.97$, slope $= 30.6°$/count). The learning trajectory parallels children's developmental progression from subset-knowers to cardinal-principle knowers. These findings demonstrate that minimal embodiment can ground abstract concepts, improve data efficiency, and yield interpretable representations aligned with biological cognition, which may contribute to embodied mathematics tutoring and safety-critical industrial applications.
2604.11373.paper_summary_zh: 機器人越來越多地進入需要理解數量的人際互動場景。智能系統如何從感官運動經驗中獲取抽象數字概念仍然是認知科學和人工智慧中的一個基本挑戰。在這裡，我們使用一個神經網絡模型來研究具身數字學習，該模型經過訓練以通過與Franka Panda操控器的自然機器人互動進行順序計數。我們展示了具身模型在僅使用10\%的訓練數據時達到了96.8\%的計數準確率，而視覺僅基準的準確率為60.6\%。當視覺-運動對應隨機化時，這一優勢依然存在，表明具身性作為結構先驗起到了正則化學習的作用，而不是作為信息來源。該模型自發發展出生物學上合理的表徵：具有對數調諧的數字選擇單元、心理數字線組織、韋伯法則縮放和編碼數字大小的旋轉動力學（$r = 0.97$，斜率 $= 30.6°$/計數）。學習軌跡與兒童從子集知識者到基數原則知識者的發展進程相似。這些發現表明，最小的具身性可以為抽象概念提供基礎，提高數據效率，並產生與生物認知一致的可解釋表徵，這可能有助於具身數學輔導和安全關鍵的工業應用。
2604.11373.author: Zhegong Shangguan et.al.
2604.11373.authors: Zhegong Shangguan, Alessandro Di Nuovo, Angelo Cangelosi
